India’s government debt grew 25 times between 2000 and 2021. But the interest it pays actually fell from nearly 12% to under 6%.

Normally, more borrowing means higher rates. But in India, many large lenders must invest in government bonds.

Banks, EPFO, and insurance companies are required to allocate a portion of deposits, provident fund savings, and premiums to government bonds.

In fact, many of them invest even more than the minimum required.

Which means a part of your savings is helping fund government borrowing, often at lower rates than a fully free market might set.

Follow Markets by Zerodha for more.

#MarketsByZerodha #GovernmentDebt #Zerodha

India’s metro network has grown from 248 km to over 1,000 km in just a decade. But not a single metro system in the country actually makes a profit.

Building metros is extremely expensive, while ticket prices are kept low to stay affordable for commuters. This means fares rarely recover the true cost.

Even the best systems mostly cover daily operations, while construction is funded by government support and loans.

Metros survive through ads, station shops, and property development. Because their real value isn’t profit. It’s time saved, congestion reduced, and economic activity created.

Follow Markets by Zerodha for more.

#MarketsByZerodha #IndianMetros #Zerodha

India wants to build a deep-water port, airport, power plant, and a full township on Great Nicobar, growing the island from 8,000 people to 6.5 lakh residents over 30 years.

The island lies next to the Malacca Strait, which carries 30% of global trade. Its naturally deep waters can dock the world’s largest ships, potentially saving India $80 to $300 per container and reducing dependence on Colombo and Singapore.

But over a million trees could be cut, fragile forests disrupted, and the Shompen tribe’s future permanently altered.

If the project succeeds, India strengthens its position in global trade.
If it fails, the damage cannot be undone.

A new alliance of 10 countries is attempting to rebuild the global AI supply chain without China - despite China currently dominating rare earth refining and key parts of semiconductor manufacturing.

Each country brings a critical advantage, from raw materials and chipmaking to capital and energy. India’s strength lies in design and engineering talent already powering global AI development.

But rebuilding supply chains from scratch could reshape costs, geopolitics, and the future of AI itself.

#MarketsbyZerodha#ChipAlliance#Zerodha

ACs already contribute up to 50 GW of peak demand. By 2035, that could reach 180 GW — nearly as much as Germany’s total power capacity.

The key issue is efficiency. The best ACs use almost half the electricity of the cheapest models, but buying decisions are still driven by upfront price.

Stronger efficiency standards can shift the entire market — reducing pressure on the grid without slowing adoption.

#MarketsbyZerodha #PowerGrid #Zerodha
